[2018-06-14 03:01] Deleted User (Deleted User): hey! in a set that has both drake and gmech
[2018-06-14 03:01] Deleted User (Deleted User): when should i choose one over the other?
[2018-06-14 03:02] camzace: choose gmech when you run out of drakes :LongNoseLiar:
[2018-06-14 03:12] nvlx (Fate): drake is the better long term attacker
[2018-06-14 03:12] nvlx (Fate): gmech gives more initial threat, like when you need to pressure red
[2018-06-14 03:13] nvlx (Fate): i mean, technically both give 4 threat, but you need to sacrifice a forge to actually execute drake's threat
[2018-06-14 03:14] camzace: the threat is just as good though

[2018-06-14 03:35] amalloy: spam gmechs to try and breach

[2018-06-14 03:36] nvlx (Fate): ah nah, costs 1 gold

[2018-06-14 03:36] camzace: oh yeah it costs money too
[2018-06-14 03:36] camzace: i always forget that

[2018-06-14 17:28] _sakuya (Sakuya): When you do a conduit start,like p2 where it slots in naturally, do you tech into blue quickly to also get absorb?

[2018-06-14 17:58] namington: Yea natural conduit into DDB is probably the most common opening in the game by far
[2018-06-14 17:59] namington: But if blue isn't necessary (there's a red and/or green absorber better than wall, blue has no good units) then you can do something else
[2018-06-14 18:00] namington: Thing is, just DD floats 5, DA floats floats 2 and one energy, and DDC isnt great except in very green heavy sets (like xaetron/zemora type deals)
[2018-06-14 18:00] namington: Sothere aren't many efficient alternatives to DDB
[2018-06-14 18:00] namington: Which is why it's so common - it basically fits perfectly.
[2018-06-14 18:01] namington: Smthn like DD DDC DDC into red is fairly strong in xaetron sets though if blue isn't that good

[2018-06-15 06:53] _sakuya (Sakuya): how in ther world do you use thunderhead
[2018-06-15 06:56] f300xen (f0x): buy it when it deals damage and absorbs
[2018-06-15 06:59] extratricky: It's a tempo unit. The best case is when buying it forces your opponent to invest most of their resources into defense, and it completely stops the attack they already have so you can build up your own offense behind it.
[2018-06-15 07:00] extratricky: Generally you want it to not get frontlined or to get frontlined on the last turn of its lifespan, which will usually mean you buy it when your opponent is just starting to ramp up their attack.
[2018-06-15 07:00] _sakuya (Sakuya): against iso kronus stacking it seems kinda iffy then
[2018-06-15 07:01] extratricky: yep, generally burst damage is good against thunderhead
[2018-06-15 07:02] amalloy: it's fine if it gets killed on like its second turn, so long as it was doing meaningful attack and absorb until then
[2018-06-15 07:03] f300xen (f0x): if you get exploited it's not good soak, and if you get absorbed it's not good attack
[2018-06-15 07:03] f300xen (f0x): is the general heuristic

[2018-06-15 08:51] _sakuya (Sakuya): Is there a targetting priority for apollo other than shooting whatevers moat valuable
[2018-06-15 08:53] _sakuya (Sakuya): Like they have animus and gaussite symbiote
[2018-06-15 08:54] _sakuya (Sakuya): Do you kill the animus or the symbiote
[2018-06-15 08:55] f300xen (f0x): the symbiote generates more gold value per turn so you should kill it
[2018-06-15 08:55] _sakuya (Sakuya): Now what about when theyre building the symbiote and you can only target like a wall, blue/red tech, and a steelspitter
[2018-06-15 08:56] leary93: as splitter can also function as a wall
[2018-06-15 08:56] leary93: unless you can breach ofc
[2018-06-15 08:56] _sakuya (Sakuya): Is tech like a bottom priority
[2018-06-15 08:56] leary93: in which case apollo deals more than just 3 dmg as it's denying absorb
[2018-06-15 08:56] f300xen (f0x): tech is a bottom priority unless you can deny important purchases or prevent units from being activated with tech
[2018-06-15 08:57] _sakuya (Sakuya): So deny big units but not anything that could be made by just rebuying the single tech structure?
[2018-06-15 08:58] amalloy: a good rule of thumb is, don't snipe tech buildings
[2018-06-15 08:58] amalloy: sure, preventing an omega buy or something is fine, but it's not amazing if they can still spend all their gold anyway
[2018-06-15 09:02] leary93: and unless you're sniping immolites or drones, tech buildings are on the low scale of snipe value in terms of gold you're destroying
[2018-06-15 09:03] _sakuya (Sakuya): When your opponent techs do you need to respond with tech too
[2018-06-15 09:03] _sakuya (Sakuya): Like p1 does three engineers
[2018-06-15 09:04] _sakuya (Sakuya): P2 does turn 2 animus for whatever reason, or theres the uj
[2018-06-15 09:04] _sakuya (Sakuya): The one that makes blue and red every othee turn
[2018-06-15 09:05] f300xen (f0x): depends entirely on the other units
[2018-06-15 09:05] _sakuya (Sakuya): Ill dig through replays later for an example set
[2018-06-15 09:11] amalloy: @Akemi if you open DD DDE, there should be no t2 response from p2 that will make you change your mind about going DDD next. you opened that way because you thought high econ would beat a rush
[2018-06-15 09:11] amalloy: if you go DD DDE DA you're just playing a weaker version of DD DD DDA
[2018-06-15 09:12] f300xen (f0x): there's the advanced strategy of dd dde as a bluff against less experienced players in order to convince them to do dde as well
[2018-06-15 09:12] f300xen (f0x): note: never do this in a real game
[2018-06-15 09:12] amalloy: yes, finally a way for advanced players to stop losing to beginners
[2018-06-15 09:12] amalloy: thank god for the DDE bluff

[2018-06-16 19:45] swapgo: I dont think theres a hard line. It also depends on whether theres efficient soak and the quality of attackers
[2018-06-16 19:46] xujhan: Both Drones and attackers give you value every turn. Very roughly speaking a damage is worth about two gold because it can be soaked by an Engineer, so you can think of your attackers as being roughly worth two drones per damage in value. Since most attackers cost less than the corresponding 'equivalent' number of drones, attackers are better investments. The tradeoff is that your first few attackers will get absorbed and give you no value, and drones are better than attackers that do nothing.
[2018-06-16 19:46] swapgo: The reason it wor-yes the above
[2018-06-16 19:47] swapgo: If im hiding behind Infusion Grid, your first 3 Ts do nothing, so its good to have more money before investing in attack
[2018-06-16 19:47] xujhan: So, again very roughly speaking, you want to make drones until you have enough economy that you can slam out a bunch of attackers and go from ~0 damage to enough damage to get over your opponent's absorb without wasting too much attack. The longer you have attackers sitting around getting absorbed, the more value you're losing.
[2018-06-16 19:48] xujhan: If your opponent can only absorb on Wall, it doesn't take that much econ to jump from 0 to 3 and be doing real damage. If your opponent can absorb on Centurion, you need a much bigger economy to jump quickly from 0 to 6.
[2018-06-16 19:52] xujhan: Incidentally this is why you usually only want to make Animus your first tech building in sets with no good absorb. There are very few things to spend red on that aren't just attackers, and if your opponent can make a good absorber you don't want to make early attackers. Green and blue are usually more flexible in the early game.
[2018-06-16 19:52] _sakuya (Sakuya): but as long as you can equal their absorb and are getting *some* damage its fine?
[2018-06-16 19:54] xujhan: Well attacking with 6 Tarsiers into a Centurion isn't exactly great value on its own, but you have to start building your damage somewhere. If you can jump from 0 to 20 damage all in one go it's fantastic, but there aren't very many ways in the game to do that.
[2018-06-16 19:54] swapgo: Looking at you Wincer
[2018-06-16 19:55] swapgo: When big absorbers are involved and they can be gotten practically, you make attack way later
[2018-06-16 19:56] amalloy: @SwapGo wincer? i think you mean thermite core
[2018-06-16 19:56] swapgo: Like, for example in a set with Energy Matrix. You can very easily go high econ (third engineer) and still wall > Matrix in time to beat natural tarsiers, so you will end up with a money lead when you start fighting back
[2018-06-16 19:58] swapgo: Oh yeah Thermite Core
[2018-06-16 19:58] swapgo: I had a nightmare match once where we were exploiting each other's DGrids and just floated 50 attack
[2018-06-16 19:59] amalloy: you and i have different nightmares
[2018-06-16 19:59] xujhan: You can think of absorb as "killing" your opponent's first X attackers. If you spend 8RR on two Tarsiers and I spend 5B on a wall, we're on even footing damage-wise but I have more money to work with, so I'm ahead. But then if you make two more Tarsiers and I make another wall, your tarsiers are doing 2 real damage to me every turn and my walls are doing none to you, so now you're ahead.
[2018-06-16 20:01] xujhan: If you get your attackers too early you'll have to make them one-or-two at a time and your opponent will get a lot of absorb value, but if you get them too late your opponent will get an insurmountable lead; the more attackers they have, the more defense you need to make and the less money you'll have spare for your own attackers. One of the main skills you need to develop is identifying the sweet spot in the middle.

[2018-06-17 15:36] xujhan: After the Antima the game looks pretty even on the board; you've both got no real attack and ~15 drones. You have a huge wad of Engis, but deadeye means that they don't get any defensive value for ages. Going red looks like the real mistake to me, not just because there are better blue units in the set but because you leave yourself badly overteched (you banked up to 7 green before hitting the panic forcefield button). The thermite strategy would probably have worked a lot better off GMechs and Kinetic Drivers than off Tarsiers. Another plan would be to make BBD+Ferritin on your Antima turn and go into deadeyes yourself. Then you'd be in a roughly even deadeye game, but your opponent is missing 5 drone supply. That's probably a win too.

[2018-06-18 03:32] extratricky: so the major thing to mention I think is that T+perf doesn't force rhino. First, it's generally not that good to rhino against first attackers that can hold, like steelsplitter or perf, because then that first attacker just holds as absorber and they don't have to buy a new absorber, so your rhino doesn't get any value on the first turn (doesn't absorb and then doesn't attack on the next turn either). The other thing is that with perf in particular, buying rhino to protect engi and buying perf+engi put you in the same position except that you have a perf instead of rhino, and perf is generally preferable. Then you can look at the engi buy and think "well, I probably have something better to spend that 2 gold on", and so the result is that you probably just don't worry about that 1 attack.
[2018-06-18 03:34] extratricky: ampo is generally good in the same sets where high econ is good, but it's more like ampo counters high econ
[2018-06-18 03:35] _sakuya (Sakuya): is low econ ampo a thing without things like flame animus
[2018-06-18 03:36] extratricky: if you tried to go for ampo in base+1 you'd die to tarsiers and rhinos, so you need some sort of support. It doens't necessarily need to be flanimus/chrono/mobile animus. It could be something like nitrocybe/husk/blood pact. I'm not going to claim to know exactly where the line is but it can sometimes happen

[2018-06-18 09:25] f300xen (f0x): savior is actually base+1 viable
[2018-06-18 09:32] _sakuya (Sakuya): how would you play this set? fairly low econ with the hellhounds?
[2018-06-18 09:33] _sakuya (Sakuya): is it actually a good play to ferretin sac > omega splitter
[2018-06-18 09:34] crash_overlord: Depends. It's generally a vai set, but if you get vai and your opp gets thunderhead you're gonna be in for a lot of hurt
[2018-06-18 09:34] crash_overlord: I'd probably still go vai. Omegas are way too weak
[2018-06-18 09:37] _sakuya (Sakuya): Since vai is double animus would you then sink some into ferretin sacs to be able to make multiple hellhounds without investing in more blastforges
[2018-06-18 09:37] _sakuya (Sakuya): And then a followup of "how greedy or stupid would it be to cut blastforges entirely"
[2018-06-18 09:40] _sakuya (Sakuya): But think of the hellhounds
[2018-06-18 09:41] extratricky: yeah I agree with going for vai. Maybe you can even get shadowfangs afterward
[2018-06-18 09:41] extratricky: vai is particularly good against omega compared to a different big absorber like ematrix because it often doesn't work to hold two omegas to guarantee the absorb, since you threaten freezing something else and forcing an omega soak
[2018-06-18 09:42] extratricky: so if you get vai you can expect any omegas that are bought to just attack, and omega is pretty medium as far as random set attackers go
[2018-06-18 09:46] _sakuya (Sakuya): Would you buy vai off a ferretin sac and just play double animus ?
[2018-06-18 09:48] _sakuya (Sakuya): Corpus buys you discount walls but they dont absorb
[2018-06-18 09:49] extratricky: that's definitely not a discount
[2018-06-18 09:50] extratricky: you probably want forge for walls, and if you are able to buy fangs you'll be less overteched than you expect
[2018-06-18 09:50] extratricky: corpus also helps with spending excess red
[2018-06-18 09:51] extratricky: the click is more like infusion grid than wall because you don't get much immediate defense out of it (you lose 2 in the corpus and gain 3, net +1)
[2018-06-18 09:51] extratricky: but it is efficient soak when you can manage it
[2018-06-18 09:52] extratricky: the thing is that usually it's not worth overdefending for that click, so it tends to happen in situations where there's high threat (admittedly this set could be one of those cases)
[2018-06-18 09:53] _sakuya (Sakuya): Is overdefending in this case making the corpus but having enough defense that it doesnt die while you absorb onto something else
[2018-06-18 09:53] extratricky: but since there's probably going to be freeze threat (vai + brooder), corpus click can come into play because you use the corpus to defend the threat and don't lose it on defense

[2018-06-18 11:15] swapgo: I have a question about Vivid
[2018-06-18 11:15] swapgo: Im very often getting beat as p2 because player one goes for the opening 1EE, 11, DDDD
[2018-06-18 11:16] swapgo: How efficient is this opening? Does P2 have a similar opening they can use in Vivid sets?
[2018-06-18 11:16] mtanzer: yeah the p2 high econ opening is 1EE 1DD DDDD
[2018-06-18 11:16] mtanzer: both the p1 and p2 high econ openings are roughly 1 gold better than other high econ openings
[2018-06-18 11:19] swapgo: Alright, thanks, I will keep it in mind
[2018-06-18 11:21] extratricky: it's worth noting that as p1 it's only really more efficient if you're going to keep droning for a bit past that. If you follow up with 11 again then you no longer have drones and don't get value from your extra engineer
[2018-06-18 11:21] extratricky: in the p2 line you still have plenty of extra drones so you get value out of it easily
[2018-06-18 11:28] leary93: Arguably p1s high vivid is also more punishable by a fastimus
[2018-06-18 11:28] mtanzer: I guess more precisely, if you compare to p1's DD DDE DDD, the vivid line has bought a 1 gold engineer (and has less drones available to turn into vivids).  if just looking 1 turn ahead that extra energy is worth a gold already because you get to buy another vivid with it, but looking 2 or more turns ahead I guess even the DDE line would catch up by continuing to spend energy since they have more drones available to turn into vivids
[2018-06-18 11:29] mtanzer: assuming you didn't want to keep buying regular drones in the vivid line as extratricky said
[2018-06-18 11:35] extratricky: To give a bit more concrete numbers, on turn 4 you'll have 13 gold and 4 energy. If you are going to want to DD1 (+ 5 gold) or DDDD (+1 gold) it should be better than the standard line, but if you want to 11 (+9 gold) it's probably worse (unless you somehow want to DDDD on the following turn). The standard line is also much more flexible against things like early red rushes, since you can D1 instead of DDD on turn 3.
[2018-06-18 11:38] leary93: DD1 is arguably the cleanest for a blastforge
[2018-06-18 11:38] leary93: only reason I can see 11 is if you want to CB
[2018-06-18 11:39] extratricky: the last time I really compared the lines it was a set with old fuge and so they spent the 9 gold on that, but that's not a thing now
[2018-06-18 11:39] extratricky: DD1B essentially gets into the standard line with a free engineer
[2018-06-18 11:39] mtanzer: if you really want animus that turn you have to 11 as well
